source: Big 4 tax issues research, web sources June 2026

issues.title: OBBBA R&E Expensing — Section 174
issues.description: OBBBA permanently restored immediate expensing of domestic R&E costs, retroactive to 2025. Foreign R&E still requires 15-year amortization. Companies that amortized domestic R&E in 2025 may be eligible for amended returns or accounting method changes.
issues.advisor_framing: Your advisor can accelerate the Section 174 method change analysis and quantify the 2025 retroactive benefit.
issues.talking_point: Your R&D spend creates an immediate cash tax recovery opportunity from the 2025 retroactive fix. That analysis needs to happen now, before the filing window closes.

issues.title: OBBBA Bonus Depreciation — Permanent 100%
issues.description: OBBBA permanently restored 100% bonus depreciation for eligible property placed in service after January 19, 2025. Significant planning opportunity for capital-intensive businesses.
issues.advisor_framing: Your advisor can model the depreciation acceleration benefit across your asset base and coordinate with the bonus depreciation election timing.
issues.talking_point: With permanent 100% bonus depreciation, your capital investment program now has a fundamentally different tax cost basis. That changes your ROI math on projects you may have already approved.

issues.title: OBBBA 163(j) — EBITDA Business Interest
issues.description: OBBBA restored EBITDA-based (not EBIT-based) business interest deduction limitation under Section 163(j). For heavily leveraged companies or those with large depreciation, this significantly increases deductible interest.
issues.advisor_framing: Your advisor can calculate the incremental interest deduction benefit from the EBITDA restoration and identify any prior-year disallowed interest carryforwards to recapture.
issues.talking_point: The EBITDA restoration could unlock significant previously disallowed interest deductions, especially if you've been building up a Section 163(j) carryforward.

issues.title: State Tax Conformity Gaps
issues.description: Most states have not conformed to OBBBA provisions. Federal vs. state taxable income diverges on bonus depreciation, R&E expensing, and 163(j). Multi-state filers need jurisdiction-by-jurisdiction analysis.
issues.advisor_framing: Your advisor can map your state footprint against current conformity status and quantify the state-federal mismatch exposure.
issues.talking_point: Your federal tax planning just got more complicated by the state layer. Most states haven't conformed to the new bonus depreciation and R&E rules yet, so your state returns could diverge significantly from your federal position.

issues.title: Pillar Two / GloBE Global Minimum Tax
issues.description: ~140 countries have adopted the 15% global minimum tax. US-parented MNEs can use the OECD side-by-side safe harbor (Jan 2026) but must document eligibility. First GloBE Information Return (GIR) filings due June 30, 2026. ASC 740 deferred tax obligations remain regardless of safe harbor elections.
issues.advisor_framing: Your advisor can assess safe harbor eligibility, build the GIR data infrastructure, and handle ASC 740 reporting for Pillar Two impacts.
issues.talking_point: The GIR filing deadline is June 30 and the data requirements are substantial. Companies that haven't started building their GloBE information systems are already behind.

issues.title: IRS Enforcement — Audit Rate Surge
issues.description: Large corporations (assets >$250M) face audit rates tripling to 22.6% by 2026. Large partnerships face 10x audit rate increase to 1%. IRS using AI/ML for detection. CAMT, transfer pricing, R&D credits, and executive compensation are primary focus areas.
issues.advisor_framing: Your advisor can conduct a pre-audit risk assessment, strengthen documentation on the highest-risk positions, and support IRS examination management.
issues.talking_point: The IRS is tripling audit rates for companies your size. A pre-audit risk review now costs a fraction of what an exam defense costs later.

issues.title: Transfer Pricing + Tariff Convergence
issues.description: Transfer pricing is the most common large-company audit trigger globally. In 2026, the same intercompany prices are now scrutinized by both tax authorities and customs authorities with directly conflicting objectives. APAs are in demand but backlogged.
issues.advisor_framing: Your advisor can conduct a transfer pricing policy review that accounts for both tax and customs optimization, and advance an APA application before the backlog grows further.
issues.talking_point: The tariff environment just made your transfer pricing documentation twice as important — and twice as conflicted. Tax authorities want high intercompany prices; customs wants low ones. Your policy needs to be defensible to both simultaneously.

issues.title: Tariff UNICAP Capitalization
issues.description: Tariffs paid on imported inventory must be capitalized under IRC §263A UNICAP rules, not immediately expensed. Section 232 duties on pharmaceuticals (100%), steel, aluminum, and copper remain active after IEEPA/Section 122 tariffs were struck down. Potential refunds may be available.
issues.advisor_framing: Your advisor can analyze your tariff exposure under the remaining Section 232 duties, identify UNICAP capitalization impact on taxable income, and track the refund opportunity as litigation proceeds.
issues.talking_point: The tariffs your company is paying are not just a trade cost — they're creating a tax timing difference under UNICAP rules that affects your quarterly provision. And there may be a refund coming if you're tracking your entries correctly.

issues.title: Corporate Alternative Minimum Tax (CAMT)
issues.description: 15% CAMT on adjusted financial statement income (AFSI) applies to corporations with >$1B in average book income. Final regulations still pending; operating on interim guidance (Notice 2026-7). Large gap between book income and taxable income creates material CAMT exposure.
issues.advisor_framing: Your advisor can model your CAMT exposure against the interim guidance, identify optimization strategies within the AFSI calculation, and monitor final regulation developments.
issues.talking_point: If your book income exceeds $1B, you're potentially in CAMT territory. The final rules still aren't out, and companies that wait for clarity are going to have a harder time course-correcting.

issues.title: Payroll — No Tax on Tips & Overtime
issues.description: OBBBA created 'no tax on tips' and 'no tax on overtime' provisions. New W-2 codes mandatory for 2026. Payroll/timekeeping systems must be upgraded. Many states do not have matching exclusions, creating withholding complexity.
issues.advisor_framing: Your advisor can assess your payroll system readiness for the new W-2 codes and navigate the federal/state mismatch on tip and overtime exclusions.
issues.talking_point: The new no-tax-on-tips rule sounds simple but requires new W-2 tracking codes and system updates before year-end. Companies that treat this as a payroll IT problem will hit it on January 1.

issues.title: R&D Tax Credit Documentation Crackdown
issues.description: IRS updated the R&D credit form with mandatory new documentation sections effective for tax year 2026. Inadequate documentation leads to disallowance under audit. Affects technology, pharma, and manufacturing companies with R&D credit claims.
issues.advisor_framing: Your advisor can review your current R&D credit documentation against the new IRS requirements and build a defensible contemporaneous documentation system.
issues.talking_point: The IRS just made the R&D credit documentation requirements substantially more demanding for this year's returns. Companies that have been claiming credits informally are at risk of full disallowance.

issues.title: Transferable Clean Energy Credits
issues.description: 25% of Fortune 1000 now participate in the transferable clean energy credit market — up 60% from 2024. Credits from IRA (sections 45X, 45Y, 48E) can be sold to offset others' tax liability. Some curtailed under OBBBA but core credit market remains active.
issues.advisor_framing: Your advisor can evaluate your credit monetization options — whether to hold, transfer, or buy — and model the cash flow and tax impact across your credit portfolio.
issues.talking_point: The transferable credit market has grown 60% in one year. If you're generating clean energy credits you can't fully use, there's a buyer. If you need to offset tax liability, there's a seller. Your advisor structures both sides of these transactions.
